name: Banff National Park
overview: Glacial lakes and rugged peaks perfect for outdoor exploration.
description: Canada's first national park is a high-altitude sanctuary of jagged peaks and turquoise waters. Centered around the charming alpine town of Banff, the park offers visitors the chance to spot grizzly bears, hike through larch forests, and stand on the edge of the world-famous Lake Louise.

name: Santorini
overview: Iconic blue-domed churches and world-famous sunsets over the Aegean.
description: Formed by one of the largest volcanic eruptions in history, Santorini is a crescent-shaped island famous for its cliffside villages of Oia and Fira. The island's unique architecture, black sand beaches, and world-class wine culture make it a legendary destination for travelers seeking a mix of luxury and ancient Cycladic charm.

name: Kyoto
overview: Ancient temples and traditional tea houses in Japan's cultural heart.
description: Kyoto is the historical soul of Japan. With its thousands of Buddhist temples, Shinto shrines, and meticulously groomed gardens, the city offers a window into the Japan of old. From the geisha district of Gion to the golden reflection of Kinkaku-ji, Kyoto remains a sanctuary of tradition in a modern nation.

name: Ubud
overview: Lush rice terraces and spiritual rainforest retreats.
description: Ubud is the spiritual center of Bali, nestled among emerald rice paddies and steep ravines. It is a hub for traditional arts, yoga retreats, and deep-forest wellness centers. Far removed from the coastal beach scene, Ubud offers a quiet, profound connection to Balinese culture and nature.

name: Paris
overview: The City of Light, home to the Eiffel Tower and the Louvre.
description: Paris is a world capital of art, fashion, and gastronomy. Known as the City of Light, it is a living museum of grand boulevards and historic architecture. Beyond the iron lattice of the Eiffel Tower, travelers can explore the winding streets of Montmartre, the halls of the Louvre, and the chic cafes that define the French 'joie de vivre'.

name: Male Atoll
overview: Unrivaled luxury in overwater villas surrounded by turquoise waters.
description: The Maldives is a tropical paradise of pristine white-sand beaches and an amazing underwater world. Comprised of 26 ring-shaped atolls, which are made up of more than 1,000 coral islands, it is the ultimate destination for seclusion, world-class diving, and staying in iconic overwater bungalows.

name: Zermatt
overview: Car-free village at the foot of the iconic Matterhorn peak.
description: Zermatt is a mountain resort renowned for skiing, climbing and hiking. The town, at an elevation of around 1,600m, lies below the iconic, pyramid-shaped Matterhorn peak. Its main street, Bahnhofstrasse is lined with boutique shops, hotels and restaurants, and also has a lively après-ski scene.

name: Reykjavik
overview: Vibrant capital gateway to glaciers, hot springs, and auroras.
description: Reykjavik, on the coast of Iceland, is the country's capital and largest city. It is home to the National and Saga museums, tracing Iceland’s Viking history. The striking concrete Hallgrimskirkja church and rotating Perlan glass dome offer sweeping views of the sea and nearby hills.

name: Dubai
overview: Futuristic skyline with the Burj Khalifa and vast desert dunes.
description: Dubai is a city and emirate in the United Arab Emirates known for luxury shopping, ultramodern architecture and a lively nightlife scene. Burj Khalifa, an 830m-tall tower, dominates the skyscraper-filled skyline. At its foot lies Dubai Fountain, with jets and lights choreographed to music. On artificial islands just offshore is Atlantis, The Palm, a resort with water and marine-animal parks.

name: Sydney
overview: Stunning harbor views, the Opera House, and Bondi Beach surf.
description: Sydney, capital of New South Wales and one of Australia's largest cities, is best known for its harbourfront Sydney Opera House, with a distinctive sail-like design. Massive Darling Harbour and the smaller Circular Quay port are hubs of waterside life, with the arched Harbour Bridge and esteemed Royal Botanic Garden nearby.

name: Tokyo
overview: Neon-lit streets, world-class sushi, and deep-rooted traditions.
description: Tokyo is a dizzying mix of futuristic skyscrapers and quiet, traditional shrines. As a global fashion and food capital, it offers everything from the high-energy Shibuya Crossing to the peaceful Meiji Jingu Forest. It is a city where ancient tea ceremonies coexist with robot cafes and high-tech marvels.

name: Rome
overview: The Eternal City, home to the Colosseum, Vatican, and fine pasta.
description: Rome is an open-air museum where nearly 3,000 years of globally influential art, architecture, and culture are on display. From the ancient ruins of the Forum to the Renaissance grandeur of the Vatican, Rome offers a timeless journey through history, complemented by a world-class culinary scene.

name: New York City
overview: The city that never sleeps, from Times Square to Central Park.
description: New York City comprises 5 boroughs sitting where the Hudson River meets the Atlantic Ocean. At its core is Manhattan, a densely populated borough that’s among the world’s major commercial, financial and cultural centers. Iconic sites include skyscrapers like the Empire State Building and sprawling Central Park.

name: Cairo
overview: Ancient wonders meet a bustling modern metropolis on the Nile.
description: Cairo is the sprawling capital of Egypt, set on the Nile River. At its heart is Tahrir Square and the vast Egyptian Museum, a trove of antiquities including royal mummies and gilded King Tutankhamun artifacts. Nearby, Giza is the site of the iconic pyramids and Great Sphinx, dating to the 26th century BC.

name: Rio de Janeiro
overview: Breathtaking landscapes, samba beats, and iconic Christ the Redeemer.
description: Rio de Janeiro is a huge seaside city in Brazil, famed for its Copacabana and Ipanema beaches, 38m Christ the Redeemer statue atop Mount Corcovado and for Sugarloaf Mountain, a granite peak with trams to its summit. The city is also known for its sprawling favelas and its raucous Carnaval festival.

name: London
overview: Royal heritage, world-class museums, and the iconic Thames riverbanks.
description: London is a 21st-century city with history stretching back to Roman times. At its center stand the imposing Houses of Parliament, the iconic 'Big Ben' clock tower, and Westminster Abbey. Across the Thames River, the London Eye observation wheel provides panoramic views of the South Bank cultural complex and the entire city skyline.

name: Barcelona
overview: Gaudí’s masterpieces, urban beaches, and world-class tapas.
description: Barcelona, the cosmopolitan capital of Spain’s Catalonia region, is known for its art and architecture. The fantastical Sagrada Família church and other modernist landmarks designed by Antoni Gaudí dot the city. Picasso Museum and Fundació Joan Miró feature modern art by their namesakes.

name: Cape Town
overview: A mix of mountains and sea with Table Mountain and stunning vineyards.
description: Cape Town is a port city on South Africa’s southwest coast, on a peninsula beneath the imposing Table Mountain. Slowly rotating cable cars climb to the mountain’s flat top, from which there are sweeping views of the city, the busy harbor and boats heading for Robben Island.

name: Amsterdam
overview: Charming canals, historic houses, and world-class museums.
description: Amsterdam is the Netherlands’ capital, known for its artistic heritage, elaborate canal system and narrow houses with gabled facades, legacies of the city’s 17th-century Golden Age. Its Museum District houses the Van Gogh Museum, works by Rembrandt and Vermeer at the Rijksmuseum, and modern art at the Stedelijk.

name: Prague
overview: The City of a Hundred Spires, with gothic bridges and medieval charm.
description: Prague, capital city of the Czech Republic, is bisected by the Vltava River. Nicknamed “the City of a Hundred Spires,” it's known for its Old Town Square, the heart of its historic core, with colorful baroque buildings, Gothic churches and the medieval Astronomical Clock, which gives an animated hourly show.

name: Venice
overview: A city built on water, known for gondolas and Renaissance architecture.
description: Venice, the capital of northern Italy’s Veneto region, is built on more than 100 small islands in a lagoon in the Adriatic Sea. It has no roads, just canals – including the Grand Canal thoroughfare – lined with Renaissance and Gothic palaces. The central square, Piazza San Marco, contains St. Mark’s Basilica, which is tiled with Byzantine mosaics.

name: Lisbon
overview: Hilly, coastal capital known for Fado music and custard tarts.
description: Lisbon is one of the oldest cities in the world, and the oldest in Western Europe. It is a city of seven hills, characterized by its yellow trams, mosaic pavements, and the melancholic sounds of Fado. From the historic Belem district to the narrow alleys of Alfama, Lisbon blends traditional heritage with striking modernism.

name: Machu Picchu
overview: Ancient Inca citadel set high in the Andes Mountains.
description: Machu Picchu is an Incan citadel set high in the Andes Mountains in Peru, above the Urubamba River valley. Built in the 15th century and later abandoned, it’s renowned for its sophisticated dry-stone walls that fuse huge blocks without the use of mortar, intriguing buildings that play on astronomical alignments and panoramic views.

name: Buenos Aires
overview: The Paris of the South, famous for tango and steak.
description: Buenos Aires is Argentina’s big, cosmopolitan capital city. Its center is the Plaza de Mayo, lined with stately 19th-century buildings including Casa Rosada, the iconic, balconied presidential palace. Other major attractions include Teatro Colón, a grand 1908 opera house with nearly 2,500 seats, and the modern MALBA museum, displaying Latin American art.

name: El Chalten
overview: The trekking capital of Argentina, gateway to Mount Fitz Roy.
description: El Chaltén is a village in Santa Cruz Province, Argentina. It is located within the Los Glaciares National Park at the base of Cerro Torre and Cerro Fitz Roy mountains, both popular for climbing. For this reason, it was named Argentina's Trekking Capital.

name: Serengeti National Park
overview: Famed for the Great Migration and world-class safaris.
description: The Serengeti is Africa's most famous park, a vast rolling plain that hosts the largest terrestrial mammal migration in the world. It is the definitive safari destination, where the 'Big Five' roam free across golden grasslands under endless blue skies. Staying here offers a raw, profound connection to the rhythm of the natural world.

name: Marrakesh
overview: A sensory feast of souks, palaces, and desert landscapes.
description: Marrakesh, a former imperial city in western Morocco, is a major economic center and home to mosques, palaces and gardens. The medina is a densely packed, walled medieval city dating to the Berber Empire, with mazelike alleys where thriving souks (marketplaces) sell traditional textiles, pottery and jewelry.

name: Maui
overview: The Valley Isle, known for pristine beaches and volcanic peaks.
description: Maui is an island in the Central Pacific, part of the Hawaiian archipelago. Sprawling Haleakala National Park encompasses the island’s highest peak, volcanic Mt. Haleakala, plus the pools and waterfalls of Ohe’o Gulch, accessed via scenic, winding Hana Highway. The island's 30 miles of beaches include golden-sand Kapalua and the windsurfing hub Kanaha Beach Park.

name: Las Vegas
overview: The entertainment capital of the world, famous for casinos and nightlife.
description: Las Vegas, often known simply as Vegas, is the 25th-most populous city in the United States. It is a world-renowned major resort city, known primarily for its gambling, shopping, fine dining, entertainment, and nightlife. The Las Vegas Valley as a whole serves as the leading financial, commercial, and cultural center for Nevada.

name: Vancouver
overview: A coastal city where mountains meet modern high-rises.
description: Vancouver is a major city in western Canada, located in the Lower Mainland region of British Columbia. As the most populous city in the province, it is also one of the most ethnically and linguistically diverse cities in Canada, surrounded by mountains and a thriving art, theater and music scene.

name: Istanbul
overview: Where East meets West across the beautiful Bosphorus.
description: Istanbul is a major city in Turkey that straddles Europe and Asia across the Bosphorus Strait. Its Old City reflects cultural influences of the many empires that once ruled here. In the Sultanahmet district, the open-air Hippodrome was for centuries the site of chariot races, while the iconic Hagia Sophia features a soaring 6th-century dome and rare Christian mosaics.

name: Agra
overview: Home to the Taj Mahal, the world's greatest monument to love.
description: Agra is a city on the banks of the Yamuna river in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is a major tourist destination because of its many Mughal-era buildings, most notably the Taj Mahal, Agra Fort and Fatehpur Sikri, all three of which are UNESCO World Heritage Sites.

name: Cappadocia
overview: Unique cave dwellings and famous hot air balloon skies.
description: Cappadocia is a stunning area in central Turkey best known for its unique 'fairy chimney' rock formations, ancient cave dwellings, and expansive underground cities. The region’s surreal moon-like landscape is most famously viewed from a hot air balloon at sunrise, drifting over the valleys of Goreme and Uchisar.

name: Ha Long Bay
overview: Towering limestone pillars and emerald waters.
description: Ha Long Bay, in northeast Vietnam, is known for its emerald waters and thousands of towering limestone islands topped by rainforests. Junk boat tours and exploration by kayak take visitors past islands named for their shapes, including Stone Dog and Teapot islets. The region is popular for scuba diving, rock climbing and hiking, particularly in mountainous Cat Ba National Park.

name: Bora Bora
overview: Breathtaking turquoise lagoons and volcanic peaks.
description: Bora Bora is a small South Pacific island northwest of Tahiti in French Polynesia. It's surrounded by a sand-fringed motu (islet) and a turquoise lagoon protected by a coral reef, it’s known for its scuba diving. It's also a popular luxury resort destination where some guest bungalows are perched over-the-water on stilts. At the island's center rises Mt. Otemanu, a 727m dormant volcano.

name: Gold Coast
overview: Stunning beaches, world-class surfing, and vibrant theme parks.
description: The Gold Coast is a metropolitan region south of Brisbane on Australia’s east coast. It's famed for its long sandy beaches, surfing spots and elaborate system of inland canals and waterways. It’s also home to theme parks such as Dreamworld, Sea World and Wet’n’Wild. Inland, hiking trails traverse Lamington National Park’s mountain ridges and valleys, home to rare birds and rainforest.

name: Cairns
overview: Gateway to the Great Barrier Reef and ancient tropical rainforests.
description: Cairns is considered the gateway to Australia's Great Barrier Reef, a city in tropical North Queensland. Its Tjapukai Aboriginal Cultural Park tells the stories of indigenous Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people with music and dance. The Cairns Esplanade, lined with bars and restaurants, has a swimming lagoon. Northwest of the city, Daintree National Park spans mountainous rainforest, gorges and beaches.

name: Edinburgh
overview: A city of cobblestone streets, hilltop castles, and legendary festivals.
description: Edinburgh is Scotland's compact, hilly capital. It has a medieval Old Town and elegant Georgian New Town with gardens and neoclassical buildings. Looming over the city is Edinburgh Castle, home to Scotland’s crown jewels and the Stone of Destiny. Arthur's Seat is an imposing peak in Holyrood Park with sweeping views, and Calton Hill is topped with monuments and memorials.

name: Nairobi
overview: The only city in the world with a National Park within its borders.
description: Nairobi is Kenya’s capital city. In addition to its urban core, the city has Nairobi National Park, a large game reserve known for breeding endangered black rhinos and home to giraffes, zebras and lions. Next to it is the well-regarded elephant orphanage operated by the David Sheldrick Wildlife Trust. Nairobi is also often used as a jumping-off point for safari trips elsewhere in Kenya.

name: Lima
overview: The culinary capital of South America, perched on the Pacific cliffs.
description: Lima is the capital and largest city of Peru. It is located in the valleys of the Chillón, Rímac and Lurín rivers, in the central coastal part of the country, overlooking the Pacific Ocean. Lima is known as the gastronomic capital of the world, with a unique blend of coastal history, colonial architecture, and modern cliffside development.

name: Auckland
overview: The City of Sails, set between two harbors and 48 volcanic cones.
description: Auckland is New Zealand's largest city and a multi-cultural hub of food, music, and commerce. Known as the City of Sails, it offers a unique lifestyle where you can live in a modern skyscraper but be on a pristine beach or a volcanic island within 30 minutes. Its geography is defined by two major harbors and dozens of dormant volcanic cones providing panoramic city views.

name: Interlaken
overview: The adventure capital of Europe, situated between two alpine lakes.
description: Interlaken lies in the Bernese Oberland on an alluvial plain, between Lake Thun and Lake Brienz. Overlooked by the three mighty mountains, Eiger, Mönch and Jungfrau, it is the starting point for numerous activities. It is famous as the adventure capital of the continent, offering paragliding, skydiving, and extreme mountain trekking.

name: Amalfi Coast
overview: Vertical colorful villages clinging to the cliffs above the blue Mediterranean.
description: The Amalfi Coast is a 50-kilometer stretch of coastline along the southern edge of Italy’s Sorrentine Peninsula, in the Campania region. It’s a popular holiday destination, with sheer cliffs and a rugged shoreline dotted with small beaches and pastel-colored fishing villages. The coastal road between the port city of Salerno and cliffside Sorrento winds past grand villas, terraced vineyards and lemon groves.

name: Florence
overview: The cradle of the Renaissance, overflowing with world-class art.
description: Florence, capital of Italy’s Tuscany region, is home to many masterpieces of Renaissance art and architecture. One of its most iconic sights is the Duomo, a cathedral with a terracotta-tiled dome engineered by Brunelleschi and a bell tower by Giotto. The Galleria dell'Accademia displays Michelangelo’s “David” sculpture, while the Uffizi Gallery exhibits Botticelli’s “The Birth of Venus” and da Vinci’s “Annunciation.”

name: Marrakesh
overview: A sensory feast of souks, palaces, and desert landscapes.
description: Marrakesh, often called the 'Red City', is a sensory explosion. Its heart is the Medina, a UNESCO World Heritage site filled with maze-like souks, historic riads, and the vibrant Jemaa el-Fnaa square. Between its stunning Islamic architecture, lush gardens like the Majorelle, and the nearby Atlas Mountains, it is Morocco’s most enchanting destination.

name: Auckland
overview: The City of Sails, set between two harbors and 48 volcanic cones.
description: Auckland is New Zealand's largest city and a multi-cultural hub of food, music, and commerce. Known as the City of Sails, it offers a unique lifestyle where you can live in a modern skyscraper but be on a pristine beach or a volcanic island within 30 minutes. Its geography is defined by two major harbors and dozens of dormant volcanic cones providing panoramic city views.

Overlooked by the three mighty mountains, Eiger, Mönch and Jungfrau, it is the starting point for numerous activities. It is famous as the adventure capital of the continent, offering paragliding, skydiving, and extreme mountain trekking.

description: The Amalfi Coast is a 50-kilometer stretch of coastline along the southern edge of Italy’s Sorrentine Peninsula, in the Campania region. It’s a popular holiday destination, with sheer cliffs and a rugged shoreline dotted with small beaches and pastel-colored fishing villages. The coastal road between the port city of Salerno and cliffside Sorrento winds past grand villas, terraced vineyards and lemon groves.

name: Florence
overview: The cradle of the Renaissance, overflowing with world-class art and leather crafts.
description: Florence, capital of Italy’s Tuscany region, is home to many masterpieces of Renaissance art and architecture. One of its most iconic sights is the Duomo, a cathedral with a terracotta-tiled dome engineered by Brunelleschi and a bell tower by Giotto. The Galleria dell'Accademia displays Michelangelo’s “David” sculpture. The Uffizi Gallery exhibits Botticelli’s “The Birth of Venus” and da Vinci’s “Annunciation.”

description: Marrakesh, often called the 'Red City', is a sensory explosion. Its heart is the Medina, a UNESCO World Heritage site filled with maze-like souks, historic riads, and the vibrant Jemaa el-Fnaa square. Between its stunning Islamic architecture, lush gardens like the Majorelle, and the nearby Atlas Mountains, it is Morocco’s most enchanting destination.
